title: Tagged / reply-to message recall
current_state: When user replies to a previous Pi message in Telegram (quote/tag), Pi cannot 'see' the quoted text. It treats the user's new reply as a standalone message. Result: tagged images get re-analysed (wasting vision tokens), tagged text loses its context.
structural_problem: Telegram exposes reply_to_message on every reply event but Pi's handler discards it. The bubble layer (T-122) preserves it in reply_targets — we just need to do something with that.
target_state: memory/recall.py exports recall_referenced(text) -> list[L3Entry]. For each text in bubble.reply_targets, embed it, search L3 + L2 (cosine > 0.85). Hits are injected into the bubble's context as 'You previously wrote: <recalled text>'. If a hit corresponds to an image analysis, the analysis text is included directly — no re-vision.
files_affected: tools/tools_telegram.py — bubble consumer calls recall_referenced before dispatch
design_notes: Reuse T-080 embedding dedup machinery (sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2)
Search order: L3 first (smaller, hotter), then L2 if no L3 hit
Cache hits in-process for the bubble lifetime to avoid duplicate searches
On miss, log via track_silent('recall.miss', context={text_prefix: <first 50 chars>}) — informational only
success_criteria: Replying to a Pi photo-analysis message recalls the analysis text
Vision is NOT re-called when the recall hits
Sub-200ms per recall lookup on a 1K-row L3
risk_notes: False positive: high-cosine hit on the wrong record. Mitigation: include the recalled text's importance and created_at in the context so the model can sanity-check. Cost: embedding every reply adds ~50ms; negligible.
migration_plan: 2. Unit tests with synthetic L3 corpus
3. Wire into Telegram bubble consumer: build context block from recall results
4. Integration test: simulate reply-to with known L3 content; assert context contains it
